title: Global Artivism 2026 Remote Opportunities: Paid Global Roles for Artists, Activists, Cultural Organizers, and Movement Builders Worldwide
summary: Global Artivism has announced multiple remote global opportunities for 2026, offering paid roles for cultural organizers, activists, researchers, campaign coordinators, and operational professionals across different world regions. These opportunities are designed for individuals deeply committed to social justice, cultural transformation, ecological integrity, and collective liberation. Led by Global Majority communities, Global Artivism is building a worldwide movement that harnesses the power of arts and culture to drive systemic change. The organization is seeking talented professionals and community-rooted changemakers to support major initiatives such as Global Artivism Month, regional ecosystem mapping, movement coordination, and global cultural activism campaigns. The available opportunities include: Admin Coordinator Global Artivism Month Specialist Regional Fellow (Africa) Regional Fellow (Americas Caribbean) Regional Fellow (Asia) Regional Fellow (Europe) Regional Fellow (Middle East / West Asia) Regional Fellow (Oceania / Pacific) These positions are fully remote and open globally or regionally depending on the role. Most opportunities prioritize candidates from the Global South and Global Majority communities. About Global Artivism Global Artivism is a Global Majority-led movement that uses arts and culture as powerful tools for justice, liberation, and systems change. The movement believes that art is not separate from activism but is instead an active force that can: Shift narratives Mobilize communities Influence public consciousness Build solidarity Reshape systems of power Global Artivism connects artists, activists, cultural workers, organizers, and movement builders across multiple regions of the world. The movement is governed by a Global Council representing diverse regions and communities globally. Core Principles of Global Artivism The organization’s work is guided by several foundational principles: Global Majority Leadership and South–South Solidarity Distributed Power and Fluid Leadership Artists at the Center, Culture as Strategy Intersectional and Community-Centered Organizing Trust Before Scale Emergence as Method Transparency and Accountability These principles shape every aspect of the organization’s programming, campaigns, collaborations, and operational structure. 1. Admin Coordinator Opportunity 2026 Global Artivism is hiring an experienced administrative professional to support the Movement Secretariat through coordination, scheduling, logistics, and communication management. Contract Details Compensation: USD $1,500–$2,500/month Location: Remote (Global) Priority: Candidates from the Global South Duration: June 1, 2026 – May 31, 2027 Type: Independent Contractor Key Responsibilities The Admin Coordinator will: Coordinate meetings across multiple time zones Support virtual and in-person event logistics Draft correspondence and manage documentation Organize schedules and action items Maintain systems across Google Workspace, Slack, Zoom, and project management tools Coordinate travel arrangements and meeting logistics Qualifications Applicants should demonstrate: Strong organizational and administrative experience Ability to support distributed international teams Excellent communication skills in English Experience with digital collaboration platforms High discretion and professionalism Alignment with Global Artivism values Community organizing and grassroots movement support experience are valued equally alongside formal administrative employment. Learn More Here 2. Global Artivism Month Specialist 2026 This role focuses on leading the delivery of Global Artivism Month, a major flagship programme planned for October/November 2026. Contract Details Compensation: USD $3,500–$5,000/month Location: Remote (Global) Duration: June 1 – December 31, 2026 Type: Independent Contractor Key Responsibilities The specialist will: Coordinate Global Artivism Month campaign delivery Build campaign toolkits and partner resources Manage timelines, budgets, and logistics Mobilize regional partners and community networks Facilitate steering committee engagement Lead post-campaign analysis and reporting Desired Experience Global Artivism is seeking candidates with: Strong programme management skills Campaign coordination experience Experience in social justice, arts, or cultural activism Community mobilization expertise Multilingual ability (preferred) Experience coordinating across global teams The organization strongly values applicants with Global South and movement-building experience. Learn More Here 3. Regional Fellowship Opportunities 2026 Global Artivism is also recruiting six Regional Fellows to strengthen regional Artivist ecosystems and coordinate community activation initiatives. Available Regions Regional Fellow positions are available for: Africa Americas Caribbean Asia Europe Middle East / West Asia Oceania / Pacific Fellowship Details Compensation: USD $900–$1,500/month Duration: June 1 – December 31, 2026 Type: Independent Contractor Work Format: Fully Remote Purpose of the Fellowship Regional Fellows will help: Build regional Artivist ecosystems Conduct cultural activism mapping and research Organize regional gatherings and exchanges Support Global Artivism Month planning Build solidarity across regional movements Main Responsibilities Regional Fellows will: Map artists, activists, organizations, and cultural movements Produce ecosystem reports and research outputs Convene regional gatherings and peer exchanges Support campaign coordination and storytelling Participate in cross-regional learning exchanges Qualifications Applicants should have: Strong community grounding in their region Experience in activism, organizing, or cultural work Research and facilitation skills Ability to coordinate networks and gatherings Strong written English communication Familiarity with social justice and movement ecosystems Global Artivism particularly values lived experience, grassroots work, and community leadership. Compensation Across Opportunities The programme offers competitive contractor fees depending on the role: Role Compensation Admin Coordinator USD $1,500–$2,500/month Global Artivism Month Specialist USD $3,500–$5,000/month Regional Fellows USD $900–$1,500/month All positions are independent contractor engagements. Contractors are responsible for their own taxes, registrations, and compliance requirements within their countries. What Global Artivism Offers Selected participants will receive: Access to a global network of Artivists and changemakers Professional development opportunities Participation in international movement-building spaces Flexible remote work arrangements Cross-regional collaboration opportunities Exposure to global arts and justice ecosystems Regional Fellows will additionally join a cohort of fellows from six global regions. How to Apply Applicants must send their applications via email to: team@globalartivism.ca Application Deadline May 17, 2026 at midnight SAST (South Africa Standard Time). Required Documents Applicants must submit: A CV (maximum two pages) A short written response (300–400 words) addressing role-specific questions One reference with contact information Shortlisted candidates will be contacted by May 25, 2026 and invited to interview rounds. Why These Opportunities Matter Global Artivism’s 2026 opportunities are more than jobs or fellowships. They are invitations to participate in a growing global movement that recognizes culture and creativity as central tools for justice and transformation. These opportunities are particularly valuable for: Artists working in activism spaces Community organizers Cultural strategists Social justice advocates Movement builders Researchers focused on arts and social change The organization’s emphasis on Global Majority leadership and community-rooted experience makes these opportunities especially inclusive for underrepresented voices and grassroots leaders globally. Discover more Job , Fellowship , and Leadership Development opportunities on OFY. The post Global Artivism 2026 Remote Opportunities: Paid Global Roles for Artists, Activists, Cultural Organizers, and Movement Builders Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Foresight Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Global Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists, Engineers, and Technology Innovators
summary: The Foresight Institute has officially opened applications for the highly prestigious Foresight Fellowship 2027 , a year-long international fellowship designed to support exceptional early-career scientists, engineers, researchers, entrepreneurs, and innovators working on transformative technologies. The fellowship provides selected participants with access to world-class mentorship, career development support, global research networks, technical workshops, funding connections, and exclusive opportunities to collaborate with some of the leading thinkers shaping the future of science and technology. Focused on accelerating breakthrough innovation, the Foresight Fellowship is ideal for ambitious individuals seeking to make meaningful contributions in areas such as artificial intelligence, neurotechnology, biotechnology, nanotechnology, space innovation, and existential risk reduction. About the Foresight Fellowship The Foresight Fellowship is a globally recognized program aimed at empowering visionary early-career innovators by connecting them with the networks, mentorship, and resources necessary to advance frontier technologies. The program is built on the belief that talented individuals can make transformative contributions when given early access to: Senior scientific mentorship Collaborative research communities Funding networks Knowledge exchange platforms High-impact scientific ecosystems The fellowship supports participants in developing both their technical expertise and long-term career trajectories. What the Fellowship Offers Selected Fellows receive extensive professional and research support throughout the year-long program. Key Benefits of the Fellowship 1. Access to Senior Scientists and Mentors Fellows are introduced to: Leading researchers Industry experts Experienced mentors Influential funders Frontier technology innovators These connections help Fellows strengthen their work and accelerate career growth. 2. Paid Participation in Major Scientific Events The fellowship includes: Attendance at technical workshops Participation in the Vision Weekend conference Fully funded travel support These events gather influential leaders working at the cutting edge of science and technology. 3. Membership in Specialized Seminar Groups Fellows gain access to: Research seminars Knowledge-sharing communities Opportunities to present their work Collaborative learning environments This allows participants to receive feedback and engage with experts in their field. 4. Priority Access to AI Research Hubs Fellows receive priority access to Foresight Institute AI Nodes located in: San Francisco Berlin These hubs support researchers and innovators working on AI for science and AI safety. 5. Career Counseling and Long-Term Community Support The fellowship also offers: Quarterly career counseling calls Guidance from industry leaders Long-term alumni network access Continued collaboration opportunities Participants become part of the broader Foresight Fellowship Alumni community after completing the program. Focus Areas of the Fellowship The fellowship supports projects and innovators working across several frontier science and technology domains. 1. Secure AI Areas include: Computer security AI alignment Secure multiagent systems AI safety research 2. Longevity Biotechnology Focus areas include: Research automation Healthcare innovation Reversible biostasis Longevity science 3. Nanotechnology Applicants may work on: Molecular 3D printing Molecular simulation Advanced nanomaterials Nanotechnology modeling systems 4. Neurotechnology Projects may involve: Brain-computer interfaces Whole brain emulation Neuro-inspired AI systems Cognitive enhancement technologies 5. Existential Hope The fellowship also supports work in: Technological alignment Strategic foresight Existential opportunity Future worldbuilding Global resilience 6. Space Innovation The fellowship welcomes innovators developing technologies and systems related to space exploration and sustainability. Who the Fellowship Is Looking For The Foresight Fellowship seeks highly motivated individuals with strong technical capabilities and ambitious visions for the future. Ideal Candidates Should Demonstrate 1. Technical Excellence Applicants should have: Strong academic or professional achievements Research publications Innovative projects Laboratory experience Proven technical expertise 2. Ambitious Vision The fellowship prioritizes “moonshot” thinkers who: Challenge conventional thinking Pursue transformative ideas Aim to solve major global problems Develop futuristic solutions 3. Commitment to Growth Successful candidates are expected to demonstrate: Intellectual curiosity Continuous learning Passion for innovation Desire to collaborate and expand their capabilities 4. Potential for Societal Impact The program strongly values projects capable of generating meaningful positive impact on society and humanity’s future. Why This Fellowship Matters The Foresight Fellowship stands out because it combines: Mentorship Scientific collaboration Career development Networking Frontier research exposure Unlike traditional fellowships, the program offers a highly flexible environment with no minimum work requirement, focusing instead on empowering exceptional individuals to maximize their potential. The fellowship creates opportunities for participants to accelerate groundbreaking work capable of shaping the future of science and technology globally. Networking and Community Opportunities Participants become part of an international community of: Scientists Engineers Entrepreneurs AI researchers Biotech innovators Futurists Technology builders This collaborative ecosystem helps Fellows exchange ideas, develop partnerships, and gain visibility for their work. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply for the 2027 cohort through the official Foresight Fellowship application portal. Official Website: Foresight Fellowship Official Website Applicants are encouraged to prepare detailed information showcasing: Their technical work Research achievements Future vision Potential societal impact Innovative projects Apply HERE Final Thoughts The Foresight Fellowship 2027 presents an exceptional opportunity for early-career innovators seeking to work at the forefront of transformative science and technology. By offering mentorship, research exposure, global networking opportunities, funded event participation, and long-term career support, the fellowship empowers visionary individuals to accelerate breakthrough innovations that could shape humanity’s future. Scientists, engineers, entrepreneurs, and researchers with bold ideas and strong technical foundations should strongly consider applying for this prestigious global fellowship. For more information about the opportunity, visit the official website Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post Foresight Fellowship 2027: Fully Supported Global Fellowship for Early-Career Scientists, Engineers, and Technology Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Zayed Sustainability Prize 2027: Global Call for Innovative Sustainability Solutions with US$7.2 Million Prize Fund
summary: The prestigious Zayed Sustainability Prize has officially opened submissions for its 2027 awards cycle , inviting innovators, nonprofit organisations, enterprises, and schools from around the world to submit transformative sustainability solutions that can improve lives and address urgent global challenges. Now in its 18th year, the Zayed Sustainability Prize has become one of the world’s most influential sustainability awards, recognizing groundbreaking initiatives that drive positive social, environmental, and economic impact. With an increased total prize fund of US$7.2 million , the 2027 cycle presents a major opportunity for changemakers working in sustainability sectors to scale their impact and gain global recognition. About the Zayed Sustainability Prize Established in 2008, the Zayed Sustainability Prize was created to honor the legacy and vision of the late Sheikh Zayed bin Sultan Al Nahyan, whose commitment to sustainability and humanitarian development continues to inspire global action. The Prize supports organizations and schools that develop innovative and scalable solutions capable of addressing some of the world’s most pressing sustainability challenges. Since its inception, the Prize has empowered global innovators whose work has significantly improved lives across communities worldwide. Prize Categories The Zayed Sustainability Prize recognizes excellence across several critical sustainability sectors. Categories Include 1. Health Solutions improving healthcare access, affordability, and health outcomes. 2. Food Innovations promoting food security, sustainable agriculture, and nutrition. 3. Energy Projects advancing clean energy access and renewable energy solutions. 4. Water Initiatives addressing water scarcity, sanitation, and safe drinking water access. 5. Climate Action Solutions focused on climate resilience, environmental protection, and carbon reduction. 6. Global High Schools Student-led sustainability projects implemented within educational institutions. Why the Zayed Sustainability Prize Matters The Prize is globally respected because it not only recognizes innovation but also helps amplify impact. The initiative supports projects capable of: Improving livelihoods Expanding access to essential services Protecting the environment Building climate resilience Supporting sustainable development goals It particularly emphasizes scalable solutions that can create measurable long-term impact across communities. Global Impact of the Prize Through its network of winners and finalists, the Zayed Sustainability Prize has already achieved remarkable global outcomes. Impact Achievements Include Over 400 million lives transformed More than 1.39 billion tonnes of CO₂ emissions reduced Over 11.4 million people gaining access to safe drinking water More than 17 million people gaining access to nutritious food Approximately 841,300 jobs created More than 1.2 million people gaining access to affordable healthcare Over 480,660 community members benefiting from high school sustainability projects These achievements demonstrate the Prize’s role in accelerating sustainable innovation worldwide. Who Should Apply? The Prize welcomes applications from: Startups Small and medium enterprises (SMEs) Nonprofit organisations Social enterprises Educational institutions and high schools Applicants are encouraged to submit projects that are: Innovative Scalable Impact-driven Sustainable Community-centered What Makes a Strong Application? Successful submissions typically demonstrate: Clear sustainability impact Measurable social or environmental outcomes Innovation and originality Scalability and long-term viability Potential for global or regional replication The Prize particularly values solutions that address urgent global challenges while creating lasting benefits for communities. Awards Cycle Timeline The 2027 cycle follows a structured timeline: Stage Timeline Submissions Open January 2026 Submissions Close June 2026 Shortlisting of Applications August 2026 Finalists Announced September 2026 Awards Ceremony January 2027 Benefits of Winning the Prize Winning or becoming a finalist in the Zayed Sustainability Prize offers far more than financial support. Key Benefits Include 1. Global Recognition Winners gain international visibility and credibility within the sustainability sector. 2. Financial Support The Prize provides significant funding to help scale impactful solutions. 3. Networking Opportunities Participants connect with: Global sustainability leaders Policymakers Investors Development organizations Innovation ecosystems 4. Expanded Impact Winning projects often experience accelerated growth, enabling broader implementation and outreach. Focus on Sustainability Innovation The Prize aligns strongly with global sustainability priorities, including: Climate action Renewable energy access Food security Water sustainability Healthcare innovation Community resilience By supporting innovative changemakers, the initiative contributes to advancing global sustainable development efforts. How to Apply Interested applicants should prepare detailed submissions highlighting: The problem being addressed The innovation or solution Demonstrated impact Scalability potential Sustainability strategy Application Deadline Submissions for the 2027 cycle close in: 22 June 2026 Apply Here Final Thoughts The Zayed Sustainability Prize 2027 stands as one of the most influential global sustainability awards available today. With millions of dollars in funding, worldwide recognition, and a strong track record of supporting impactful solutions, the Prize provides an extraordinary platform for innovators committed to improving lives and protecting the planet. Organizations, enterprises, nonprofits, and schools working on transformative sustainability initiatives should strongly consider applying before the June 2026 submission deadline. For more information visit the official website Discover more global grant opportunities on OFY, VISIT The post Zayed Sustainability Prize 2027: Global Call for Innovative Sustainability Solutions with US$7.2 Million Prize Fund appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) 2026: Apply for Up to €5,000 in Funding for Youth-Led Community Impact Projects Worldwide
summary: Applications are now officially open for the 2026 Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF), an international micro-grant initiative designed to support young changemakers and youth-led initiatives creating meaningful social impact in communities around the world. The Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) provides young people with access to non-repayable funding, global visibility, and support networks to help transform innovative community ideas into practical, sustainable solutions. The initiative is part of the broader Global Youth Mobilization movement, a global platform led by major youth organizations in partnership with international institutions focused on empowering youth action and leadership. Through this funding opportunity, young leaders can access up to €5,000 in grant funding to implement projects addressing pressing social, environmental, educational, and developmental challenges. About the Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) The Global Youth Empowerment Fund is a youth-focused funding initiative aimed at: Supporting youth-led community solutions Expanding access to funding for young innovators Encouraging grassroots social impact Promoting youth participation in sustainable development Strengthening local and global youth leadership The fund recognizes that many young people have innovative ideas and practical solutions for challenges within their communities but often lack access to the financial support necessary to implement them. The programme helps bridge that gap by providing: Micro-grant funding Visibility opportunities Global youth networking Capacity-building support Community impact resources About Global Youth Mobilization The Youth Empowerment Fund is part of the Global Youth Mobilization initiative, a global movement led by youth organizations and international partners focused on: Youth empowerment Community transformation Sustainable development Social innovation Global youth participation The initiative supports youth-driven solutions to some of the world’s most pressing challenges. What the Fund Supports The Youth Empowerment Fund supports projects focused on solving urgent local and global challenges in areas such as: 1. Community Development Projects that: Improve local communities Promote inclusion and empowerment Address social challenges Support vulnerable populations 2. Climate Action Initiatives focused on: Environmental sustainability Climate awareness Green innovation Community resilience Environmental advocacy 3. Education and Skills Development Projects that: Expand learning opportunities Improve youth education Build employability skills Promote digital literacy Support vocational development 4. Health and Wellbeing Initiatives supporting: Mental health awareness Public health education Community wellbeing Youth health interventions 5. Social Inclusion and Equality Projects promoting: Gender equality Youth inclusion Diversity and representation Human rights and equity Why the Youth Empowerment Fund Matters Young people across the world are increasingly leading innovative social and environmental solutions, yet many face: Limited access to funding Lack of mentorship opportunities Insufficient visibility Resource barriers Challenges scaling their ideas The YEF seeks to remove these barriers by empowering youth-led initiatives with practical support and funding. The programme places young people at the center of community transformation and recognizes youth as critical drivers of sustainable change. Grant Value Successful applicants may receive: Up to €5,000 in Non-Repayable Funding The micro-grant funding is intended to support the design, implementation, and growth of youth-led community impact projects. Benefits of the Programme Selected participants and organizations will gain access to several valuable opportunities. 1. Financial Support Participants receive funding to implement and expand their projects. 2. Global Visibility Projects gain increased international exposure and recognition. 3. Access to a Global Youth Network Participants become connected to an international ecosystem of youth leaders and changemakers. 4. Capacity-Building Opportunities Applicants may gain access to: Training resources Support materials Knowledge-sharing opportunities Community engagement support 5. Social Impact Development The programme helps youth-led projects: Scale their impact Strengthen sustainability Improve community engagement Who Can Apply? The Youth Empowerment Fund is open to: Individuals Youth groups Youth-led organizations Informal youth initiatives Young social innovators Applicants must: Be between 14–30 years old Lead youth-centered or community-focused initiatives Demonstrate commitment to social impact Present innovative project ideas Eligible Project Types The fund welcomes a broad range of youth-led initiatives, including: Climate action campaigns Education projects Health awareness programmes Social inclusion initiatives Community development interventions Youth empowerment activities Sustainability innovations Digital inclusion projects Why This Opportunity Is Important The Youth Empowerment Fund is more than a financial grant programme. It represents: Recognition of youth leadership Investment in grassroots innovation Support for local problem-solving Expansion of youth participation in development The programme encourages young people to move from ideas to action by providing the support needed to create measurable impact. Application Deadline Applicants are strongly encouraged to submit their applications before: 22nd May, 2026 Given the high global interest expected for this opportunity, early applications are highly recommended. Application Process Interested applicants should: Visit the official application portal Prepare details of their project idea Explain their community impact strategy Provide information about their team or organization Submit the completed application before the deadline Click HERE to submit an application Tips for a Strong Application Applicants can improve their chances by: Clearly explaining the community problem they want to solve Demonstrating measurable impact potential Showing strong youth leadership involvement Presenting realistic implementation plans Highlighting sustainability and long-term value Emphasizing innovation and creativity Click HERE to learn more about the Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) 2026. Discover more global Grant opportunities on OFY HERE . The post Global Youth Empowerment Fund (YEF) 2026: Apply for Up to €5,000 in Funding for Youth-Led Community Impact Projects Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Amplifying Young Voices for a Sustainable Future: FAO & AUN-AFS Research Proposal Competition for Youth 2026 Opens Applications
summary: The Food and Agriculture Organization of the United Nations (FAO), in collaboration with the ASEAN University Network for Agriculture and Food Security (AUN-AFS), has officially launched the FAO AUN-AFS Research Proposal Competition for Youth 2026, a transformative regional initiative designed to empower young researchers and master’s students to contribute innovative solutions toward building sustainable and resilient agrifood systems. Announced during a landmark webinar held on 23 April 2026, this competition aims to bridge the gap between academic research and practical policy implementation by encouraging youth-led ideas, scientific innovation, and evidence-based solutions that address pressing food security and agricultural challenges in the Asia-Pacific region. The initiative is open to eligible master’s students and young researchers from ASEAN countries, China, Japan, and South Korea, providing a unique opportunity for participants to showcase their research capabilities while receiving financial and academic support to advance their projects. Why the FAO AUN-AFS Research Proposal Competition Matters Agrifood systems across the world are currently facing multiple challenges, including climate change, food insecurity, environmental degradation, and socioeconomic inequalities. Young people are increasingly recognized as key actors in creating sustainable solutions that can transform agriculture and food systems for future generations. Through this competition, FAO and AUN-AFS aim to: Encourage youth participation in agrifood systems transformation Promote innovative research that aligns with FAO regional priorities Support evidence-based policymaking Foster collaboration between universities, researchers, and international organizations Strengthen food security and sustainability across the region Build leadership skills among young scholars and researchers The competition represents a strategic investment in the next generation of researchers, innovators, and policymakers who will contribute to a healthier and more resilient global food system. Financial and Academic Support for Selected Projects One of the major highlights of the competition is that selected research proposals will receive financial support to help participants maximize the impact and quality of their research projects. This support is intended to help young researchers develop practical, scalable, and policy-relevant solutions capable of contributing to sustainable agrifood systems in the region. Beyond financial assistance, participants will also gain access to valuable academic resources and learning opportunities. Access to FAO eLearning Academy Participants in the competition will benefit from access to the FAO eLearning Academy, FAO’s globally recognized digital learning platform offering UN-certified courses across multiple technical areas related to agriculture, nutrition, food security, sustainability, and rural development. The eLearning Academy will help participants: Strengthen technical expertise Improve research methodologies Develop evidence-based solutions Understand FAO regional priorities Consolidate research perspectives Enhance academic and professional skills The platform contains extensive research-based learning materials that can guide participants in refining their research ideas before transforming them into impactful proposals. Opportunity to Participate in the 10th International Agriculture Congress (IAC 2026) As part of the initiative, FAO, Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM), and AUN-AFS will organize an Academia Roundtable during the 10th International Agriculture Congress (IAC 2026), which will be hosted by UPM in Malaysia. This event will provide finalists with the opportunity to: Engage in regional dialogue on agrifood systems Network with experts, researchers, and policymakers Share innovative ideas and perspectives Develop leadership and communication skills Gain exposure in an international academic environment The roundtable aims to create a collaborative atmosphere that encourages youth leadership, intellectual exchange, and innovative thinking. Statements from Organizers and Partners According to Prof. Dr. Loh Teck Chwen, Dean of the Faculty of Agriculture at Universiti Putra Malaysia, the collaboration between FAO and AUN-AFS creates meaningful opportunities that support: Youth engagement Innovation Food security Agrifood systems transformation He emphasized the importance of investing in the next generation of researchers and leaders who will help shape a healthier and more sustainable future. Ms. Kaori Abe, Head of Partnerships at the FAO Partnerships and UN Collaboration Division, also highlighted the importance of collaboration between FAO, universities, academic networks, and students in addressing urgent regional and global challenges. She stressed that young people across the region possess extraordinary talent, innovation, and commitment, and that collaborative action can transform potential into impactful solutions for people, the planet, and future generations. Who Can Apply? The competition is open to: Master’s students Young researchers Applicants must come from eligible universities within: ASEAN member countries China Japan South Korea Interested applicants are encouraged to consult the official list of eligible universities before submitting their applications. Deadline Applications must be submitted before: Sunday, 31 May 2026 Late applications may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to prepare and submit their proposals early. How to Apply Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Amplifying Young Voices for a Sustainable Future: FAO AUN-AFS Research Proposal Competition for Youth 2026 Opens Applications appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Ghana TVET Service Recruitment 2026: Apply for Teaching, Administrative, and Support Staff Positions Across Ghana
summary: The Ghana TVET Service Recruitment 2026 portal is officially open, offering a major employment opportunity for qualified professionals, instructors, administrators, and support staff who want to contribute to technical and vocational education across Ghana. The Ghana TVET Service (GTVET) is responsible for recruiting and posting trained and qualified personnel to public technical and vocational institutions nationwide. The 2026 recruitment cycle covers all 16 regions of Ghana and includes opportunities in more than 230 accredited TVET institutions. This nationwide recruitment exercise aims to strengthen Ghana’s technical and vocational education system by hiring competent professionals who can help develop a skilled workforce for the future. The recruitment process is fully online, transparent, and structured to ensure equal opportunity for qualified applicants. About the Ghana TVET Service Recruitment Portal The Ghana TVET Service Recruitment Portal serves as the official platform for all TVET recruitment activities in Ghana. Through this portal, applicants can: Register online Purchase recruitment vouchers Upload required documents Apply for vacancies Track application progress Receive appointment letters digitally The Ghana TVET Service has emphasized that all postings, appointments, and transfers are managed exclusively through the official recruitment portal. Applicants are strongly advised to avoid unofficial recruitment channels and scams. Categories of Vacancies Available The 2026 recruitment cycle includes opportunities in three major categories: Teaching Staff Teaching positions are available for: Tutors Facilitators Senior Tutors Technical Instructors Technical Assistants Vacancies cover multiple technical and vocational disciplines, including: Engineering ICT Hospitality Fashion Building and Construction Agriculture Technical Education Applied Sciences These professionals will play a vital role in training students with practical and industry-relevant skills. Administrative Staff Administrative positions include: Managers Assistant Managers Senior Assistants These staff members will oversee institutional administration, operations, policy implementation, and management functions within TVET institutions. Support Staff Support roles include: Matrons Assistant Matrons Catering Staff Security Guards Drivers Labourers Cleaners These positions are essential for maintaining the smooth operation of TVET institutions nationwide. Nationwide Coverage Across Ghana The Ghana TVET Service recruitment covers: All 16 regions of Ghana Over 230 accredited TVET institutions More than 50 training programmes This large-scale recruitment exercise demonstrates Ghana’s commitment to strengthening technical and vocational education nationwide. Why the Ghana TVET Recruitment Matters Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET) plays a critical role in national development by equipping learners with practical skills required in modern industries and labor markets. The Ghana TVET Service seeks professionals who can help: Improve technical education delivery Enhance workforce development Promote innovation and entrepreneurship Build industry-relevant competencies Support youth employment initiatives Strengthen institutional administration Successful applicants will contribute directly to shaping Ghana’s future workforce. Eligibility Requirements for Applicants Applicants must meet all stated eligibility requirements before submitting an application. Ghanaian Citizenship Applicants must: Be Ghanaian citizens Hold legal authorization to work in Ghana Possess a valid Ghana Card number The Ghana Card will be used during profile verification. Educational Qualifications Requirements vary depending on the position category. Teaching Class Positions Applicants for Tutor or Senior Tutor positions must possess: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in: Technology Engineering Education Professional teaching qualification Technical Instructor Positions Applicants must hold: Technician Part I, II, or III Certificate OR HND/Tertiary Diploma in Technology or Engineering Administrative Class Positions Applicants require: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in: Education Engineering Management Business Administration Public Administration Social Sciences Catering Class Positions Applicants should possess: Bachelor’s degree HND Advanced Certificate in: Catering Hospitality Management Home Economics Security, Driver, and Labourer Positions Requirements include: WASSCE/SSSCE or BECE Relevant licenses where applicable Physical fitness requirements National Service Completion Applicants for Teaching, Administrative, and Catering positions must possess a valid National Service certificate issued by the National Service Authority. Professional Teaching Qualification Applicants for teaching positions must possess recognized education qualifications such as: PGDE (Postgraduate Diploma in Education) Equivalent teaching certification CBT certification is considered an added advantage for senior teaching roles. Work Experience Requirements Experience requirements depend on the position level. Examples include: Senior Tutors and Matrons: Minimum 1 year experience Senior Assistants and Driver Grade I: Minimum 3 years experience Advanced Security and Driver grades: Up to 6 years experience Entry-level positions may not require prior experience. Medical Fitness and Clean Record Applicants must: Be medically fit Meet physical fitness requirements where applicable Have no false qualification declarations Possess a clean criminal record False declarations may result in disqualification and possible prosecution. Step-by-Step Application Process The recruitment process consists of five phases. Phase 1: USSD Payment and Voucher Access Applicants must: Dial the official USSD shortcode Pay the application fee Receive a voucher code via SMS The voucher remains valid throughout the recruitment cycle. Phase 2: Profile Creation and Document Upload Required documents include: Ghana Card Academic certificates Transcripts National Service certificate Professional certificates Passport-size photograph All documents must be certified true copies. Phase 3: Application Submission Applicants will: Browse vacancies by region Upload CV and cover letter Answer job-specific questions Submit applications online Phase 4: Screening and Verification The Ghana TVET Service will conduct: Document verification Qualification assessments Background checks Candidate shortlisting Successful candidates will be contacted via SMS and the portal dashboard. Phase 5: Posting and Appointment Successful applicants will: Receive institutional postings Download official appointment letters Report to Regional Directors Advantages of the Online Recruitment System The recruitment system offers several benefits: 100% online application process 24/7 portal access Digital appointment letters Easy registration and application tracking Transparent recruitment procedures Important Notice to Applicants The Ghana TVET Service has emphasized that: Only shortlisted applicants will be contacted All credentials will be verified with issuing institutions Incomplete applications may not be processed False information may lead to prosecution Applicants are advised to carefully review all requirements before applying. Official Application Portal Interested applicants can access the official recruitment portal here: Ghana TVET Service Recruitment Portal Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post Ghana TVET Service Recruitment 2026: Apply for Teaching, Administrative, and Support Staff Positions Across Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: United Nations JPO Opportunities 2026: Apply for UNDP Human Rights Analyst in Malawi and IFAD Partnership Analyst in Rome
summary: The United Nations system continues to provide transformative global career opportunities for young professionals through its prestigious Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme. Two remarkable opportunities currently attracting international attention are the UNDP Human Rights Analyst position in Malawi and the IFAD Partnership Analyst (Member State Engagement) position in Rome, Italy. These highly competitive international positions offer young professionals the opportunity to gain hands-on experience within the United Nations system while contributing to global development, human rights, governance, rural transformation, resource mobilization, and sustainable development goals. The positions are designed for ambitious early-career professionals who aspire to build long-term careers in multilateral organizations and international development. About the United Nations JPO Programme The Junior Professional Officer (JPO) Programme is one of the most respected entry pathways into the United Nations and international development institutions. The programme equips talented young professionals with: International work experience Professional mentorship Technical and leadership training Exposure to multilateral cooperation Opportunities to contribute to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) Career development support Networking opportunities with global experts and policymakers JPOs work under the supervision of experienced international professionals and actively contribute to the design, implementation, monitoring, and evaluation of development programmes. The programme also helps participants strengthen technical expertise, leadership abilities, strategic thinking, and intercultural communication skills. UNDP Human Rights Analyst JPO Position – Lilongwe, Malawi The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) is recruiting a Human Rights Analyst under the Junior Professional Officer Programme to support governance, rule of law, and human rights initiatives in Malawi. Position Details Job Title: Human Rights Analyst Organization: UNDP Duty Station: Lilongwe, Malawi Grade Level: P2 Contract Type: FTA International (JPO) Contract Duration: 1 year, renewable subject to performance and agreement Bureau: Regional Bureau for Africa (RBA) Career Stream: Governance / Policy and Programme About UNDP Malawi’s Governance Programme UNDP Malawi supports the country’s national development agenda and the Sustainable Development Goals through initiatives focused on: Human rights Governance Rule of law Gender equality Peacebuilding Civic engagement Institutional accountability Democratic participation The Governance Portfolio aligns with Malawi 2063 and Sustainable Development Goals 5 and 16. The programme focuses on three major pillars: Pillar I – Effective, Accountable and Responsive Institutions This pillar strengthens governance systems, accountability mechanisms, and public institutions. Pillar II – Civic Engagement and Empowerment This area promotes citizen participation, inclusion, and democratic engagement. Pillar III – Oversight, Human Rights and Rule of Law This pillar supports justice institutions, anti-corruption mechanisms, human rights promotion, and the protection of vulnerable populations. Focus on Vulnerable Groups and Human Rights One of the defining aspects of the Human Rights Analyst role is its emphasis on promoting and protecting the rights of vulnerable groups, including: LGBTQI populations Women and girls Children Persons with disabilities Refugees and asylum seekers Persons affected by climate change Persons living with HIV/AIDS Persons with albinism Older persons UNDP works closely with the Malawi Human Rights Commission and other institutions to strengthen inclusive governance and equal access to justice. Key Responsibilities of the UNDP Human Rights Analyst The selected candidate will support: Human Rights and Rule of Law Programming Responsibilities include: Supporting treaty body reporting mechanisms Monitoring human rights violations Supporting justice and security institutions Coordinating human rights programming Preparing project documents and work plans Monitoring programme implementation Supporting donor reporting Strategic Partnerships and Resource Mobilization The JPO will: Engage with civil society organizations Coordinate with development partners Support policy dialogue Organize workshops and consultations Identify opportunities for programme expansion Knowledge Management and Research Additional duties include: Producing analytical briefs Supporting monitoring and evaluation Documenting lessons learned Researching international best practices Qualifications Required for the UNDP Position Applicants must possess: Education Master’s degree or equivalent in: Law International Human Rights Development Studies Social Sciences Related disciplines Experience Minimum 2 years of relevant professional experience Experience with human rights institutions or government institutions Experience in programme planning and reporting Strong Microsoft Office skills Desired Skills Experience in international human rights systems Experience promoting rights of vulnerable groups Strong analytical and communication abilities Language Requirement Fluency in English is mandatory Learning and Career Development Opportunities at UNDP The selected JPO will benefit from: UNDP induction programmes Mentorship opportunities Masterclasses and professional training Career development support Networking with UN professionals Duty-related travel and training allocation The programme is specifically designed to prepare young professionals for future international careers within the UN system. IFAD Partnership Analyst (Member State Engagement) – Rome, Italy The International Fund for Agricultural Development (IFAD) is also recruiting a Junior Professional Officer for the position of Partnership Analyst (Member State Engagement) based in Rome, Italy. About IFAD IFAD is a specialized United Nations agency and international financial institution dedicated to eradicating rural poverty and hunger worldwide. The organization invests in rural communities and supports initiatives aimed at: Increasing agricultural productivity Improving rural incomes Strengthening food systems Supporting rural transformation Promoting sustainable development About the IFAD External Relations Department (ERD) The selected JPO will work within the Global Engagement, Partnership and Resource Mobilization Division (GPR) of IFAD’s External Relations Department. This division focuses on: Partnership building Resource mobilization Global policy engagement Strategic communications Donor relations International cooperation Responsibilities of the IFAD Partnership Analyst The selected candidate will support IFAD’s collaboration with donor countries and strategic partners. Main responsibilities include: Partnership Development Identifying new collaboration opportunities Supporting strategic engagement with Member States Building partnerships with foundations, corporations, and development actors Resource Mobilization Supporting donor engagement Monitoring funding opportunities Maintaining partnership databases Supporting replenishment efforts Policy Engagement and Outreach Organizing workshops and conferences Preparing briefing notes and talking points Supporting high-level meetings and international events Strategic Analysis Conducting research on development cooperation Analyzing donor priorities Monitoring global policy trends Focus Areas: Mattei Plan for Africa and Global Gateway The position specifically supports collaboration linked to: Italy’s Mattei Plan for Africa The European Union’s Global Gateway initiative These initiatives aim to strengthen sustainable investment, development partnerships, and infrastructure cooperation across Africa. Qualifications Required for the IFAD Position Education Applicants must possess an advanced university degree in: International Development International Relations Political Science Public Policy Economics Business Management Social Sciences Related disciplines Experience Minimum 2 years of progressively relevant experience Experience in partnership development or resource mobilization Experience in multicultural environments is highly desirable Desired Skills Partnership building Stakeholder engagement Strategic communication Advocacy Data management Problem-solving Diplomacy and negotiation Language Requirement Excellent English required French, Spanish, or Arabic considered an advantage Learning Opportunities at IFAD The selected JPO will benefit from: On-the-job training Coaching from senior professionals International missions Exposure to donor engagement processes Experience in global development financing Training organized by IFAD and external providers The programme also offers strong exposure to international development diplomacy and multilateral cooperation. Why These Opportunities Are Important Both positions offer exceptional exposure to international development work and represent valuable pathways into long-term careers within the United Nations system. Successful applicants will gain: International experience Exposure to global policymaking Professional mentorship Multilateral development expertise Leadership and communication skills Opportunities to contribute to sustainable development These opportunities are especially ideal for young professionals passionate about: Human rights Governance Rural development Food security International cooperation Policy analysis Sustainable development Apply here Learn More For more information about this opportunity, Click here More global Job opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here The post United Nations JPO Opportunities 2026: Apply for UNDP Human Rights Analyst in Malawi and IFAD Partnership Analyst in Rome appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Postdoctoral Researcher Opportunity at Concordia University: Immigration, Public Opinion and Integration Research Position in Canada
summary: Researchers and professionals in migration studies and behavioural sciences now have an exciting opportunity to advance their academic and professional careers through a prestigious postdoctoral research position at Concordia University. The “Immigration, Public Opinion and Integration” research opportunity is being offered under the “Bridging Divides” initiative, a growing research platform focused on understanding migration, newcomer integration, and public attitudes toward immigration in modern societies. This opportunity is particularly valuable for scholars interested in social sciences, migration policy, behavioural research, immigrant integration, and public opinion studies. The selected candidate will work closely with researcher Antoine Bilodeau and his dynamic research team at Concordia University in Canada. The application deadline for this opportunity is May 15, 2026 . About the Research Opportunity As migration continues to shape economies, cultures, and political systems across the world, understanding immigrant integration and public attitudes toward immigration has become increasingly important. This postdoctoral position is designed to support high-level academic research that contributes to evidence-based policy discussions and social understanding. The selected postdoctoral researcher will contribute to projects that explore how immigrants adapt to new societies, how citizens perceive immigration, and how migration experiences influence integration outcomes. The position offers researchers the chance to work within a collaborative academic environment while engaging in meaningful and impactful social science research. Key Research Areas The successful applicant will participate in advanced research related to several important themes, including: 1. Public Opinion on Immigration The researcher will investigate societal attitudes toward immigration and migrants, including: Public perceptions of newcomers Political and social attitudes toward immigration policies Media influence on immigration discourse Social cohesion and multiculturalism debates Factors influencing support or opposition to immigration Understanding public opinion is critical for governments, policymakers, and organizations seeking to build inclusive societies and reduce social polarization. 2. Immigrant Integration Another major focus of the research is immigrant integration into host societies. Areas of study may include: Economic integration Social inclusion Cultural adaptation Educational outcomes Labour market participation Community engagement Barriers faced by immigrants and refugees The researcher may also examine how institutions and public policies influence integration outcomes for newcomers. 3. Life Experiences and Migration Trajectories of Newcomers This aspect of the research focuses on the human experiences behind migration statistics. The selected scholar may study: Migration journeys and settlement experiences Identity formation among immigrants Challenges faced during resettlement Long-term integration trajectories Experiences of refugees and vulnerable migrants Social mobility and adaptation over time These studies can contribute to more humane and informed migration policies globally. Why This Opportunity Stands Out This postdoctoral opportunity provides several advantages for researchers looking to expand their academic and professional impact. Benefits of the Position Opportunity to work with a respected research team at Concordia University Exposure to high-impact migration and integration research Access to advanced quantitative research projects Collaboration with leading scholars in migration and behavioural studies Potential contribution to academic publications and policy discussions Valuable international research experience in Canada Networking opportunities within the global migration research community Ideal Candidate Profile The position is best suited for highly motivated researchers with strong academic and analytical backgrounds. Applicants are expected to demonstrate: Expertise in migration studies and/or behavioural studies Experience in advanced quantitative research methods Strong social science research skills Ability to work collaboratively within research teams Interest in immigration, integration, and public opinion research Excellent analytical and communication abilities Candidates with previous experience in survey analysis, statistical modeling, or migration-focused research may have an added advantage. Why Migration Research Matters in the Mid-21st Century Migration is one of the defining global issues of the modern era. Countries worldwide are experiencing demographic changes, labour mobility, refugee movements, and multicultural transformations. Research initiatives such as “Bridging Divides” play an important role in: Reducing misinformation about immigrants Supporting evidence-based immigration policies Promoting social cohesion Understanding integration challenges Encouraging inclusive societies Informing public debate with credible research This makes the position more than just an academic role — it is an opportunity to contribute to meaningful societal conversations and policy development. About Concordia University Concordia University is one of Canada’s recognized research institutions, known for innovation, interdisciplinary collaboration, and global engagement. Located in Montreal, the university provides an intellectually stimulating environment for researchers from around the world. Researchers joining Concordia gain access to: Diverse academic networks Research funding opportunities International collaborations Advanced research infrastructure A multicultural academic environment Montreal itself is widely regarded as one of the leading student and research cities in North America. Application Deadline Applicants interested in this postdoctoral research opportunity should submit their applications before: Deadline: May 15, 2026 Early applications are strongly encouraged. How to Apply Interested applicants can learn more about the position and submit their applications Here Final Thoughts The Postdoctoral Researcher position on Immigration, Public Opinion and Integration at Concordia University represents an outstanding opportunity for social science researchers passionate about migration studies and behavioural research. With migration continuing to influence global societies, this role offers scholars the chance to contribute to impactful research that can shape policy, improve public understanding, and support immigrant integration efforts worldwide. Researchers with strong quantitative skills and a passion for migration-related topics should consider applying before the May 15, 2026 deadline. For more global job opportunities Click here . The post Postdoctoral Researcher Opportunity at Concordia University: Immigration, Public Opinion and Integration Research Position in Canada appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: APWLD Uprising / Art Rising Feminist Artivism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded $3,000 Opportunity for Feminist Artists in Asia and the Pacific
summary: The Asia Pacific Forum on Women, Law and Development (APWLD) has officially opened applications for the 2026 Uprising / Art Rising: Feminist Artivism Fellowship, a unique regional opportunity designed for artists committed to feminist movements, social justice, and transformative storytelling through art. This groundbreaking fellowship invites artists from across Asia and the Pacific to collaborate with feminist activists, grassroots organisations, and regional movements to create powerful artistic works that amplify women’s lived realities and challenge structural inequalities. Selected fellows will receive a stipend of USD 3,000, mentorship, political grounding, regional exposure, and the opportunity to showcase their creative work in Bangkok, Thailand as part of the Uprising / Art Rising: A Feminist Art Space. The fellowship is ideal for artists working in visual arts, theatre, film, digital media, interdisciplinary arts, and other creative formats who are passionate about feminist advocacy, climate justice, labour rights, economic justice, food sovereignty, migration, and peace-building. About APWLD The Asia Pacific Forum on Women, Law and Development is one of the leading feminist networks in Asia and the Pacific. APWLD is a regional feminist, membership-driven organisation with more than 313 members from 31 countries and territories across Asia and the Pacific region. The organisation is committed to: Advancing women’s human rights Building feminist movements Promoting Development Justice Challenging structural inequalities Supporting grassroots activism Strengthening feminist solidarity across borders Through research, advocacy, movement-building, and creative engagement, APWLD works to address systemic issues affecting women and marginalised communities. About the Uprising / Art Rising Fellowship The Uprising / Art Rising: Feminist Artivism Fellowship was developed as an expansion of APWLD’s successful collaboration with artists and activists during the 2025 multidisciplinary exhibition titled “On Debt Row: A Multidisciplinary Art Exhibition on Debt and Its Impact on Lives in the Global South.” That exhibition, held in Sevilla, Spain, explored how debt and economic inequality affect women’s lives and communities across the Global South. Building on this success, APWLD is now launching a broader regional fellowship program that combines: Art activism Feminist movement-building Political education Grassroots collaboration Public engagement Creative advocacy The fellowship aims to support artists in co-creating meaningful art that reflects women’s voices, struggles, resistance, and lived experiences. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will gain access to extensive support and opportunities throughout the fellowship period. Financial Support Each selected fellow or team will receive: USD 3,000 stipend for project production and related expenses The funding will support creative work based on proposals co-developed and approved with APWLD. Additional Fellowship Benefits Participants will also receive: Political grounding and feminist analysis training Mentorship from APWLD programmes and partners Access to grassroots feminist organisations Regional networking opportunities Participation in feminist advocacy spaces Opportunities to attend APWLD activities and events Regional and international exposure for their work Exhibition opportunities in Bangkok, Thailand Promotion of their work through APWLD’s media and advocacy platforms Fellowship Duration The fellowship will run from: June 2026 to November 2026 Throughout this period, fellows will actively collaborate with APWLD and feminist movements across the region. Themes and Focus Areas APWLD is seeking artistic projects that creatively interpret pressing feminist and social justice issues. Applicants may focus on one or more of the following themes: 1. Feminist Economic Justice Artists may explore: Public and private debt Austerity measures Privatisation of public services Women’s livelihoods Resistance to illegitimate debt 2. Climate Justice Possible focus areas include: Feminist climate leadership Grassroots environmental movements Energy transitions Environmental defence Extractivist economies Corporate resistance to climate action 3. Peace and Justice Artists may address: Militarism Surveillance Occupation Repression of women human rights defenders Threats to feminist movements 4. Food Sovereignty Potential topics include: Agroecology Indigenous food systems Land rights Community food autonomy Resistance against industrial agriculture Corporate capture of food systems 5. Feminist Trade Justice Applicants may creatively explore: Trade systems Corporate power Women’s labour Economic inequality Human rights and trade intersections 6. Labour and Migration Projects may focus on: Women migrant workers Labour exploitation Migration systems Workers’ rights Gendered labour experiences Who Can Apply? The fellowship is open to artists from Asia and the Pacific region working in: Visual arts Theatre Film and video Digital arts Interdisciplinary arts Feminist creative activism Both individual and team applications are accepted. However, teams will still receive the same total stipend amount of USD 3,000. What Fellows Will Do Selected fellows will undertake a wide range of creative and collaborative activities during the fellowship. Key Responsibilities Include: Creating at least one major creative work or artwork Participating in virtual orientation and political grounding sessions Collaborating with APWLD programmes and grassroots partners Attending relevant APWLD events and advocacy spaces Travelling to Bangkok, Thailand in October 2026 Participating in the Uprising / Art Rising: A Feminist Art Space Submitting a documentation and financial report Producing social media content including: Photos Creative process updates One-minute video reels Participating in a final reflection meeting in November 2026 What is the Uprising / Art Rising: A Feminist Art Space? One of the most exciting aspects of the fellowship is participation in the Uprising / Art Rising: A Feminist Art Space in Bangkok. This physical gathering serves as: A collaborative feminist art space A site for experimentation and healing A platform for feminist storytelling A regional exhibition venue A community-building initiative The event showcases artistic works that amplify women’s voices and strengthen feminist struggles across Asia and the Pacific. Mentorship and Political Education A defining feature of this fellowship is its emphasis on political grounding and feminist analysis. Fellows will receive training and engagement opportunities around: Patriarchy Globalisation Fundamentalisms Militarism Feminist Development Justice Feminist movement-building Structural inequalities Each fellow will also be paired with APWLD programmes and partners relevant to their chosen theme. Selection Criteria Applicants will be evaluated based on: 1. Artistic Background Applicants must demonstrate experience in: Feminist art Social justice storytelling Human rights advocacy Grassroots movement engagement 2. Quality of Proposed Project Selection will consider: Creativity Feasibility Relevance to feminist themes Advocacy potential Community impact 3. Commitment to Feminist Movements Applicants should demonstrate alignment with feminist movement-building and social justice principles. 4. Language Skills Applicants must be: Proficient in English Able to communicate in at least one Asia-Pacific regional language 5. Travel Capacity Applicants must be able to travel internationally if required. Required Application Documents Interested applicants must submit: Completed application form Curriculum Vitae (CV) Project pitch/proposal Budget plan Sample work/portfolio Why This Fellowship Matters The APWLD Feminist Artivism Fellowship is more than a funding opportunity. It is a transformative platform that combines: Feminist politics Creative resistance Movement-building Regional solidarity Public advocacy Artistic freedom The fellowship empowers artists to challenge oppressive systems, amplify marginalised voices, and contribute meaningfully to feminist struggles across Asia and the Pacific. For artists committed to social justice and creative activism, this fellowship offers a rare opportunity to merge art, advocacy, and regional collaboration in a deeply impactful way. Eligible Applicants This opportunity is suitable for: Feminist artists Visual artists Theatre practitioners Filmmakers Video artists Digital artists Interdisciplinary artists Art activists Human rights creatives Grassroots cultural workers Qualifications Required Applicants should: Have experience in creative arts practice Demonstrate commitment to feminist movement-building Have experience working on women’s rights or development issues Be proficient in English Speak at least one Asia-Pacific regional language Be able to travel internationally if required Application Deadline 28 May 2026 How To Apply Click here for more and to apply Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid missing the deadline. For more global fellowship opportunities Click here The post APWLD Uprising / Art Rising Feminist Artivism Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded $3,000 Opportunity for Feminist Artists in Asia and the Pacific appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Women in Conflict 1325 Fellowship 2026 in Scotland: Fully Detailed Peacebuilding Opportunity for Women from Conflict-Affected Countries
summary: The Beyond Borders Scotland has officially opened applications for the highly impactful Women in Conflict 1325 Fellowship Programme 2026, a transformative international fellowship focused on advancing women’s meaningful participation in peacebuilding. Organised in partnership with ELS, this prestigious fellowship will bring together women leaders, peacebuilders, activists, creatives, and advocates from conflict-affected countries for an intensive residential programme in Scotland from 20–25 July 2026. The fellowship is designed to strengthen women’s leadership in peacebuilding while creating opportunities for dialogue, solidarity, collaboration, and cross-cultural learning among participants from different regions of the world. Participants will engage in high-level discussions, workshops, collaborative learning experiences, and networking sessions focused on women’s rights, conflict resolution, peacebuilding, and social transformation. This programme is especially significant because it centers women’s voices, lived experiences, and leadership in global peacebuilding conversations while creating a supportive international network of changemakers. About Beyond Borders Scotland The Beyond Borders Scotland is a platform dedicated to small nation dialogue, cultural exchange, peacebuilding, and international cooperation. The organisation works to: Foster dialogue across borders Encourage peacebuilding initiatives Support intercultural understanding Promote leadership and collaboration Strengthen global solidarity networks Amplify underrepresented voices in international conversations Through fellowships, festivals, podcasts, and cultural engagement initiatives, Beyond Borders Scotland creates spaces for meaningful dialogue and transformative leadership development. About the Women in Conflict 1325 Fellowship The Women in Conflict 1325 Fellowship Programme is inspired by the principles of United Nations Security Council Resolution 1325 on Women, Peace, and Security. The fellowship focuses specifically on: Women’s Meaningful Participation in Peacebuilding The programme recognises that women play essential roles in: Conflict prevention Community healing Peace negotiations Human rights advocacy Social reconstruction Transitional justice Grassroots organising Policy development However, women are often excluded from formal peace processes and decision-making spaces. This fellowship seeks to challenge that exclusion by empowering women leaders and strengthening their capacity, visibility, and influence within peacebuilding efforts globally. Fellowship Theme for July 2026 The July 2026 programme will specifically focus on: “Women’s Meaningful Participation in Peacebuilding” Participants will explore the challenges, opportunities, and strategies surrounding women’s leadership and inclusion in peacebuilding initiatives worldwide. Programme Structure The fellowship includes a week-long residential programme in Scotland alongside virtual engagement opportunities before and after the residential experience. Key Components Include: Residential learning sessions Workshops and facilitated discussions Networking opportunities Collaborative dialogue Community-building activities Leadership development Peer learning Global solidarity exchanges Participants will also take part in online sessions before and after the in-person fellowship to deepen learning and sustain long-term collaboration among fellows and alumnae. What Fellows Will Experience The fellowship aims to create an immersive and transformative experience for participants. During the programme, fellows will: 1. Participate in Peacebuilding Discussions Participants will engage with diverse perspectives on: Women, Peace and Security Conflict transformation Feminist peacebuilding Community resilience Human rights advocacy Grassroots leadership International solidarity 2. Build International Networks The fellowship creates opportunities for participants to: Connect with women leaders globally Build long-term professional relationships Exchange experiences and strategies Develop solidarity across regions and sectors 3. Engage in Collaborative Learning The programme promotes mutual learning through: Shared storytelling Dialogue sessions Group reflections Cultural exchange Interactive workshops 4. Join the Fellowship Alumnae Network Participants will become part of a broader global fellowship community that continues collaboration and engagement beyond the programme itself. Who Should Apply? The programme is intended for women from conflict-affected countries who are actively involved in peacebuilding, women’s rights, or related sectors. Applicants may come from backgrounds such as: Peacebuilding Human rights advocacy Community organising Women’s rights activism Social justice movements Journalism Academia Policy work Arts and culture Conflict resolution initiatives Importantly, applicants with arts backgrounds are also strongly encouraged to apply. Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will gain access to numerous professional and personal development opportunities. Benefits Include: Participation in an international residential fellowship in Scotland Engagement with global peacebuilding experts and practitioners Leadership development opportunities Expanded international networks Cross-cultural learning experiences Long-term alumnae engagement Opportunities for solidarity-building Exposure to diverse peacebuilding approaches Collaborative learning spaces The programme also creates a supportive environment where women leaders can share experiences, challenges, and strategies for advancing peace and justice. Diversity and Inclusion Commitment The organisers strongly emphasise diversity and inclusion. Applications are welcomed from individuals of all backgrounds, and those with accessibility needs are especially encouraged to apply. The programme values: Diverse lived experiences Inclusive participation Accessibility Cross-cultural dialogue Intersectional feminist approaches Eligibility Criteria To qualify for the fellowship, applicants must: Identify as women Come from conflict-affected countries Be working in peacebuilding, women’s rights, or related fields Demonstrate commitment to social change and peacebuilding Be able to participate in the fellowship activities Applicants from arts and creative backgrounds are also eligible and encouraged to apply. Required Application Documents Applicants must submit: Completed application form Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV) Reference letter Copy of passport Applicants are encouraged to prepare all documents carefully before submission. Application Deadline Deadline: 20 May 2026 Click here for more and to apply The organisers have noted that applications may close earlier than expected if they receive an overwhelming number of submissions. Early applications are strongly encouraged. Important Notes About the Programme The fellowship includes: Pre-programme online sessions Residential programme from 20–25 July 2026 Post-programme online engagement This structure ensures continuous learning and sustained collaboration among participants. Why This Fellowship Matters Women continue to face barriers to meaningful participation in peacebuilding despite their critical contributions to conflict prevention and social transformation. The Women in Conflict 1325 Fellowship seeks to address this gap by: Elevating women’s leadership Creating global solidarity networks Strengthening peacebuilding capacity Supporting feminist approaches to peace Encouraging intercultural collaboration Amplifying underrepresented voices For women leaders committed to peace, justice, human rights, and social transformation, this fellowship offers a powerful platform for learning, collaboration, and international engagement. For more Fellowship opportunities Click here The post Women in Conflict 1325 Fellowship 2026 in Scotland: Fully Detailed Peacebuilding Opportunity for Women from Conflict-Affected Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .
